page: Amazon Web Services m8i.16xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 64 vCPUs, corresponding to 32 physical cores with hyper-threading, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE and AMX_INT8). The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This configuration does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance across multiple compute and memory workloads. The server achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 2201.0 and a multi-core score of 21499.0, placing it in the top tier of tested systems. It also demonstrated high memory bandwidth, reaching 5137.47 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 196.85 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Database and web-serving capabilities are notable, with Redis SET operations reaching 115.06 million operations per second and static web serving throughput recorded at 107.39 GB/sec. For machine learning workloads, the CPU-based AMX extensions enabled prompt processing speeds of 298.38 tokens per second for medium 7B LLM models, though text generation for large 70B models was limited to an average speed of 3.49 tokens per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m8i.16xlarge offers high resource density for general-purpose workloads, leveraging fast DDR5 memory and dedicated Intel Xeon compute without the added expense of a discrete GPU. Tradeoffs include the complete absence of local NVMe storage, requiring reliance on network-attached storage, and an average large-block memory write speed of 80.7 GB/sec. The server is suited for enterprise application hosting, web serving, database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8i.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 256.0 GB of DDR5 RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz, this x86_64 server utilizes the Nitro hypervisor and includes advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps. Benchmarks show top-tier performance, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 21499.0 and Redis SET operations reaching 115.06 million operations per second. While prompt processing for medium LLMs is strong at 298.38 tokens per second, text generation for large 70B models is a relative bottleneck at 3.49 tokens per second. This server is suited for high-throughput web serving, database operations, and CPU-based inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.16xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier database and web serving performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.16xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A general-purpose server featuring DDR5 memory and Intel Xeon processors with AMX extensions for database and CPU-based inference workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 256.0 GB of DDR5 RAM at 7 GHz
Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with AVX-512 and AMX extensions
Top-tier Redis database performance at 115.06 million operations per second
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor
Suited for enterprise web serving, databases, and CPU-based LLM inference
